title: Customer service occupations n.e.c.
description: Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. Examine, evaluate, and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations governing contract compliance of licenses and permits, and perform other compliance and enforcement inspection and analysis activities not classified elsewhere. Inspect and investigate sources of pollution to protect the public and environment and ensure conformance with Federal, State, and local regulations and ordinances. (Based on 37 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Identify interested and qualified customers to provide them with additional information, and contact regular and prospective customers to demonstrate products, explain product features, and solicit orders.
Instruct customers in the alteration of products and advise customers on product usage to improve production.
Visit trade shows, stores, community organizations, or other venues to demonstrate products or services, answer questions, and accommodate large crowds.
Learn about competitors' products or consumers' interests or concerns to provide more complete information, and research or investigate products to be presented to prepare for demonstrations.

Prepare or alter presentation contents to target specific audiences; practice demonstrations to ensure that they will run smoothly; transport, assemble, and disassemble materials used in presentations; and set up and arrange displays or demonstration areas to attract the attention of prospective customers.

Demonstrate or explain products, methods, or services to persuade customers to purchase products or use services, and provide product samples, coupons, informational brochures, or other incentives to persuade people to buy products.

Keep areas neat while working and return items to correct locations following demonstrations, and record and report demonstration-related information, such as the number of questions asked by the audience or the number of coupons distributed.
Sell products being promoted, keep records of sales, and provide customers with ongoing technical support.
Recommend product or service improvements to employers and provide feedback to product design teams so that products can be tailored to clients' needs.
Provide product information using various methods, including lectures, films, charts, or slide shows, and suggest specific product purchases to meet customers' needs.
Wear costumes or sign boards and walk in public to promote merchandise, services, or events; stock shelves with products; and open or close information facilities.

Contact customers or travel agents to advise them of travel conveyance changes or to confirm reservations, and provide customers with travel suggestions and information sources, such as guides, directories, brochures, or maps.
Provide clients with assistance in preparing required travel documents and forms, and collect samples of merchandise for examination, appraisal, or testing.
Prepare customer invoices and accept payment; indicate availability and confirm bookings for services and products related to customer needs; and maintain customer records, using automated systems.
Ensure compliance with advertising and regulatory requirements by inspecting documentation, and review facts of incidents to determine if violations occurred.
Evaluate needs of potential customers and select or assist customers in selecting products based on those needs, product specifications, and applicable regulations.
Monitor market conditions, product innovations, and competitors' products, prices, and sales; and maintain logs and prepare reports related to performance and market conditions.

Collaborate with colleagues to exchange information, such as selling strategies or marketing information, and prepare sales presentations or proposals to explain product specifications or applications.
Demonstrate the operation or use of technical or scientific products, and consult with engineers regarding technical problems with products.
Negotiate with retail merchants to improve product exposure, such as shelf positioning and advertising, and arrange and direct delivery and installation of products and equipment.

Plan, assemble, and stock product displays in retail stores, or make recommendations to retailers regarding product displays, promotional programs, and advertising, and check stock levels and reorder merchandise as necessary.
Forward orders to manufacturers and obtain credit information about prospective customers.

Perform administrative duties, such as preparing sales budgets and reports, keeping sales records, and filing expense account reports, and provide advice on transportation options, types of carriers, or shipping routes.
Estimate or quote prices, credit or contract terms, warranties, and delivery dates, and take quote requests or orders from dealers or customers.

Identify prospective customers by using business directories, following leads from existing clients, participating in organizations and clubs, and attending trade shows and conferences, and provide customers with product samples and catalogs.
Develop marketing or strategic plans for sales territories and generate solar energy customer leads to develop new accounts.
Develop or conduct employee regulatory training, and prepare or maintain technical files as necessary to obtain and sustain product approval.

Prepare proposals, quotes, contracts, or presentations for potential solar customers, and provide technical review of data or reports to be incorporated into regulatory submissions to assure scientific rigor, accuracy, and clarity of presentation.

Review product promotional materials, labeling, batch records, specification sheets, or test methods for compliance with applicable regulations and policies, and assess sites to determine suitability for solar equipment.
Monitor the implementation and impact of guidelines for nondiscriminatory employment practices, and recommend changes to company procedures in response to changes in regulations or standards.
Examine permits, licenses, applications, and records to ensure compliance with licensing requirements, and check with employers or other references to verify answers and obtain further information.

Compile, record, and evaluate personal and financial data to verify completeness and accuracy, and to determine eligibility status, and confer with or interview officials, technical or professional specialists, or applicants to obtain information or to clarify facts relevant to licensing decisions.

Interview benefits recipients at specified intervals to certify their eligibility for continuing benefits, and initiate procedures to grant, modify, deny, or terminate assistance, or refer applicants to other agencies for assistance.
Participate in internal or external audits, and prepare reports related to investigations of equal opportunity complaints and ensure conformance to regulations.
Notify the next of kin of deaths and coordinate the release of personal effects to authorized persons.
Conduct inquiries into code violations and determine actions to be taken, issuing written notices of violation as necessary, and assist clients in obtaining bonds.
Monitor market conditions, innovations, and competitors' services, prices, and sales, and inform customers of contracts or other information pertaining to purchased services.
Coordinate with or assist law enforcement agencies in matters of mutual concern, and testify in court or at administrative proceedings concerning investigation findings.
Create forms or agreements to complete sales, and distribute promotional materials at meetings, conferences, or trade shows.
Investigate claimants for the possibility of fraud or abuse and conduct annual, interim, and special housing reviews and home visits to ensure conformance to regulations.

Provide applicants with assistance in completing application forms, such as those for job referrals or unemployment compensation claims, and refer applicants to job openings or to interviews with other staff, in accordance with administrative guidelines or office procedures.
Respond to questions and inquiries, such as those concerning service charges and capacity fees, or refer them to supervisors.
Interpret civil rights laws and equal opportunity regulations for individuals or employers, and study equal opportunity complaints to clarify issues.
Coordinate, monitor, or revise complaint procedures to ensure timely processing and review of complaints, and conduct surveys to evaluate findings to determine if systematic discrimination exists.
Prepare reports of selection, survey, or other statistics and recommendations for corrective action, and prepare reports of activities, evaluations, recommendations, or decisions.

Review company contracts to determine actions required to meet governmental equal opportunity provisions, and verify that all job descriptions are submitted for review and approval and that descriptions meet regulatory standards.

Counsel newly hired members of minority or disadvantaged groups, informing them about details of civil rights laws and provide information, technical assistance, or training to supervisors, managers, or employees on topics such as employee supervision, hiring, grievance procedures, or staff development.
Report law or regulation violations to appropriate boards or agencies, and warn violators of infractions or penalties.

Advise licensees or other individuals or groups concerning licensing, permit, or passport regulations, and evaluate applications, records, or documents to gather information about eligibility or liability issues.
Investigate employment practices or alleged violations of laws to document and correct discriminatory factors, and document all investigative activities.

Inspect government property, such as construction sites or public housing, to ensure compliance with contract specifications or legal requirements, and determine which sites and violation reports to investigate.
Conduct field surveillance to gather case-related information, and gather financial documents related to investigations.
Interview individuals to determine the nature of suspected violations and to obtain evidence of violations, and interview witnesses or suspects and take statements.
Perform administrative support tasks, such as proofreading, transcribing handwritten information, and operating calculators or computers to work with various documents.
Greet persons entering establishment, determine nature and purpose of visit, and direct or escort them to specific destinations.

Provide information about the establishment, such as location of departments or offices, employees within the organization, or services provided, and explain to customers how specific types of advertising will help promote their products or services effectively.
Prepare written reports of investigation findings, and create and maintain logs, records, or databases of information about fraudulent activity.

Schedule promotional or performance engagements for clients, arrange meetings concerning issues involving their clients, and develop contacts with individuals and organizations to ensure their clients' success.
Review reports of suspected fraud to determine the need for further investigation, and recommend actions in fraud cases.
Testify in court regarding investigation findings, and prepare evidence for presentation in court.
Maintain assigned account bases while developing new accounts, and gather all relevant material for bid processes, coordinating bidding and contract approval.

Negotiate with responsible parties to arrange for recovery of losses due to fraud, and negotiate with managers, promoters, union officials, and other persons regarding clients' contractual rights and obligations.
Inform health professionals, property owners, and the public about harmful properties and related problems of water pollution and contaminated wastewater.

Prepare estimates used by management for purposes such as planning, organizing, and scheduling work, and prepare cost and expenditure statements and other necessary documentation for the duration of the project.
Maintain knowledge of current events and trends in areas such as money laundering and criminal tools and techniques, and conduct in-depth investigations of suspicious financial activity.

Collect, sort, distribute, or prepare mail, messages, or courier deliveries, and operate telephone switchboard to answer, screen, or forward calls, providing information, taking messages, or scheduling appointments.
Analyze and implement state, federal or local requirements as necessary to maintain approved pretreatment, pollution prevention, and storm water runoff programs.
Analyze data to determine answers to questions from customers or members of the public, and provide application or analytical support to researchers or traders on issues such as valuations or data.
Conduct surveys and evaluate findings to determine if systematic discrimination exists, and monitor follow-up actions in cases where violations were found, reviewing compliance monitoring reports.
Set up cost monitoring and reporting systems and procedures, and establish and maintain tendering process, including conducting negotiations.

Conduct special studies to develop and establish standard hour and related cost data or to reduce cost. Prepare and maintain a directory of suppliers, contractors, and subcontractors, and compile data pertinent to manufacture of special products for customers.

Visit site and record information about access, drainage and topography, and availability of utility services. Collect payment for transportation and accommodations from customers and compute cost of travel and accommodations, using calculators and other resources.

Converse with customers to determine destination, mode of transportation, travel dates, financial considerations, and accommodations required. Book transportation and hotel reservations, and provide customers with brochures and publications containing travel information.
Plan, describe, arrange, and sell itinerary tour packages and promotional travel incentives offered by various travel carriers.

Prepare documents and correspondence, such as damage claims, credit and billing inquiries, invoices, and service complaints. Compose letters in reply to correspondence and route correspondence to other departments for reply.

Record and maintain information on clients, vendors, and travel packages, and gather records pertinent to specific problems, review them for completeness and accuracy, and attach records to correspondence as necessary.
Maintain files and control records to show correspondence activities. Read incoming correspondence to ascertain nature of writers' concerns and determine disposition.
Compile data from records to prepare periodic reports and ensure that money collected is properly recorded and secured.

Prepare or organize online storefront marketing material, including product descriptions or subject lines, optimizing content to search engine criteria. Analyze corporate intelligence data to identify trends, patterns, or warnings indicating threats to security of people, assets, information, or infrastructure.

Correspond with online customers via electronic mail, telephone, or other electronic messaging to address questions or complaints about products, policies, or shipping methods. Calculate purchase subtotals, taxes, and shipping costs for submission to customers.
Receive and process payments from customers, using electronic transaction services. Create and maintain databases of customer accounts, and create business continuity and disaster recovery budgets.

Develop and implement security practices to preserve assets and customer privacy. Conduct or oversee contingency plan integration and operation, and develop emergency management plans for recovery decision making.
Identify opportunities for strategic improvement or mitigation of business interruption. Create scenarios to reestablish operations from various types of business disruptions.
Removed irrelevant tasks not applicable to customer service occupations.
Upload digital media, such as photos, video, or scanned images to online storefront, auction sites, or other shopping websites.
Purchase new or used items from online or physical sources for resale via retail or auction websites.
Formulate pricing policies on merchandise according to profitability requirements.
Listen to and resolve customer complaints regarding services, products, or personnel.
Prepare rental or lease agreements, specifying charges and payment procedures for use of machinery, tools, or other items.
Monitor sales staff performance to ensure that goals are met.
Attend company meetings to exchange product information and coordinate work activities with other departments.
Prepare sales and inventory reports for management and budget departments.
Examine merchandise to ensure correct pricing and display, and that it functions as advertised.
Analyze details of sales territories to assess their growth potential and to set quotas.
Keep records pertaining to purchases, sales, and requisitions.
Coordinate sales promotion activities, such as preparing merchandise displays and advertising copy.
Visit retailers and sales representatives to promote products and gather information.
Inventory stock and reorder when inventories drop to specified levels.
Examine products purchased for resale or received for storage to determine product condition.
Direct and supervise employees engaged in sales, inventory-taking, reconciling cash receipts, or performing specific services.
Plan and prepare work schedules, and assign employees to specific duties.
Provide staff with assistance in performing difficult or complicated duties.
Confer with company officials to develop methods and procedures to increase sales, expand markets, and promote business.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel.

emerging_tasks.task: Read and review subordinates' reports to ensure legal standards are met and there are no mistakes.
Log client information and sales leads into tracking documents or software.
Complete or review death certificates, including the assignment of cause and manner of death.
Develop guidelines for nondiscriminatory employment practices, such as affirmative action plans and equal opportunity employment policies.
Observe and record the positions and conditions of bodies and related evidence at the scene, through documentation or photographs.
Train employees on equal opportunity laws, guidelines, or policies, such as discrimination, diversity, harassment, or affirmative action.
Use emerging technologies, such as drones, for remote or automated inspections.
Enter and update databases of contact information, such as names, addresses, and phone numbers.
Use remote sensing technologies or drones to evaluate site conditions when in-person visits are not feasible.
Prepare written reports or presentations on findings and recommendations.
Train personnel and vendors in security procedures or use of security equipment.
